Notice Of Motion For Order Setting Aside And Vacating Default And Default Judgment And Granting Deft Leave To Defend
Off Calendar Jul-27-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT MARIA LUIS MOTION FOR ORDER SETTING ASIDE AND VACATING DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT AND GRANTING DEFENDANT LEAVE TO DEFEND. Appearances: Luis G. Molina of the Law Offices of Luis G. Molina (647.6003) for defendant and moving party. No other appearances. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: granted, proposed answer must be filed on or before July 28, 2010. Defendant's neglect excusable in view of her communications with plaintiffs and their counsel. The court favors adjudicating cases on the merits. Order signed in open court. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-29-2010 Off Calendar

Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Off Calendar Mar-23-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 218, Comm. William R. Gargano, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, CSR: Lois Jones, #3689.

Albert L. Boasberg, Esq. appeared with plaintiffs John Guerin and Amy Guerin, who were placed under oath and examined.
Defendant Maria Victoria Luis was present at today's hearing, but was not permitted to testify as a default was entered against this defendant on December 23, 2009.
Doe defendants were dismissed on January 10, 2010.

Exhibit 1---Canceled Checks from defendant to plaintiffs-----------------------------ID/EVID.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiffs John Guerin and Amy Guerin, and against the Defaulted Defendant Maria Victoria Luis as follows:

Damages/Principal - $99,000.00.
Costs - $370.00, for a total judgment in the amount of $99,370.00.

Additionally, the Court found that defendant Maria Victoria Luis committed promissory fraud upon plaintiffs John Guerin and Amy Guerin within the meaning of Civil Code Section 1710(4).

Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.
